content: Radiation side effects classified as acute (during/within 3 months) or late (>3 months post-treatment). Acute effects: fatigue (universal), skin reactions (erythema, desquamation), mucositis (head/neck, pelvic radiation), esophagitis (thoracic radiation), diarrhea (abdominal/pelvic), cytopenias (large bone marrow volumes irradiated). Managed with supportive care: topical steroids for skin, pain control for mucositis, antidiarrheals, nutritional support. Late effects: fibrosis (tissue scarring, organ dysfunction), lymphedema, xerostomia (permanent after head/neck radiation), cognitive decline (brain radiation), cardiac toxicity (left breast/chest radiation), secondary malignancies (sarcomas, carcinomas in field, latency 5-30 years). Organ-specific constraints: lung V20 <35% (pneumonitis risk), heart mean dose <26 Gy (cardiac events), spinal cord max <50 Gy (myelopathy), bowel V45 <195cc (obstruction risk). RTOG/EORTC toxicity criteria grade adverse events 1-5.
